title: Use a Debit Card Safely
summary: Use a debit card for purchases while protecting the PIN and account.
learnerPromise: You will be able to use a debit card confidently while keeping your account secure.
whyItMatters: Debit cards draw money directly from your bank account, so protecting your card and PIN helps prevent unauthorized charges and keeps your money safe.
realLifeUses: Paying for groceries at a checkout terminal without exposing your PIN
Withdrawing cash from an ATM without risking card theft
Shopping online with a debit card using trusted websites
Noticing and reporting suspicious transactions quickly
result: You know how to use a debit card securely and what to do if something goes wrong.
youWillLearn: Cover the keypad when entering your PIN
Choose credit over debit at payment terminals when possible
Check your account regularly for transactions you did not make
Know what to do if your card is lost or stolen
miniChallenge: Log into your banking app and review the last ten transactions to make sure you recognize all of them.
steps: Inspect any card reader or ATM for loose parts or unusual attachments before inserting your card.
When entering your PIN, use your free hand to shield the keypad from view.
At a store terminal, choose the credit option if prompted; this often skips the PIN and offers more fraud protection.
After each purchase, put your card away immediately and take your receipt.
Set a weekly reminder to check your account for unfamiliar transactions and report them right away if you find any.
completionCriteria: You can list three safety habits for using a debit card
You have reviewed your recent transactions and know how to report a problem
commonProblems: Skimming devices attached to ATMs or gas station pumps
Shoulder surfing where someone watches you enter your PIN
Forgetting to report a lost card quickly, leading to unauthorized use
Using debit cards on unsecured public Wi-Fi for online purchases
tips: Never share your PIN with anyone, even friends or family
If your bank offers transaction alerts, turn them on for instant notifications
Memorize your PIN instead of writing it down or storing it with your card
